65.reason: [Error: free(head) only frees the first node, but the rest of the list is leaked. Additionally, when the list has only one node, free(q) later will double-free the same node because q points to head. This causes "free(): double free detected".]

Additionally, the program does not free all allocated nodes, causing memory leaks. The double free error occurs because q is freed after head, but q may have been already freed as part of the list traversal or due to incorrect pointer management.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Double free error: if the polynomial result has only one term, head equals q. Freeing both causes double free. Also, this does not correctly free the entire linked list, leading to memory leaks or crashes if list > 1 node and q is elsewhere.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Runtime Error: Double free. If the list has only one node, head == q. Freeing head then q frees the same memory twice. Also, generally, one should free the entire linked list properly, not just head and q.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Only head is freed, but the entire linked list is not traversed and freed properly. This causes memory leak, but not double-free. However, the real double-free comes from: when a term is merged (same exponent), q is not freed, but later 'free(q)' at the end tries to free a possibly already-freed or invalid pointer. Also, q may point to a node that was already inserted and then merged, so freeing q unconditionally is wrong.]

66.reason: [If the result polynomial has only one term (as in the test case), then 'head' and 'q' point to the same allocated node. Freeing both leads to a double free, which causes the runtime error "free(): double free detected in tcache 2". Additionally, if there are multiple terms, 'q' points to the last allocated node, but intermediate nodes are not freed, and 'q' might even be NULL in edge cases (though not here). The proper way is to traverse and free the entire linked list, not just 'head' and 'q'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Runtime Error: Double free. See above. If head == q, this is a double free. If q was merged and freed earlier, this is a use-after-free/double free. If q is in the list but not head, freeing it here corrupts the list structure if other nodes point to it, or is just incorrect memory management.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This line causes double-free: if during insertion a node q was merged (i.e., same exponent found), then q was not inserted into the list and should be freed immediately (which wasn't done). But if it was inserted, then it's part of the list and will be freed when traversing the list — but here we don't traverse. Worse: q points to the last allocated node, which might have been merged and thus already logically discarded (but not freed), OR it might be in the list. In the test case, only one term is produced, so q points to the only node. Then 'free(head)' frees it, and 'free(q)' frees the same address again → double free.]
